description: How forces act during collisions and impacts.

lessons.title: What happens when two things hit each other?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 1 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 1 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: What causes changes in the motion and shape of colliding objects?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 2 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 2 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: Do all objects change shape or bend when they are pushed in a collision?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 3 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 3 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: How much do you have to push on any object to get it to deform (temporarily vs. permanently)?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 4 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 4 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: How does changing the mass or speed of a moving object before it collides with another object affect the forces on those objects during the collision?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 5 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 5 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: What have we figured out about objects interacting in collisions? How can we apply our new learning to answer questions about objects interacting in collisions?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 6 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 6 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: How much does doubling the speed or doubling the mass affect the kinetic energy of an object and the resulting damage that it can do in a collision?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 7 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 7 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: Where did the energy in our launcher system come from, and after the collisions where did it go to?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 8 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 8 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: How do other contact forces from interactions with the air and the track cause energy transfers in the launcher system?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 9 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 9 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: Why do some objects break or not break in a collision?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 10 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 10 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: What can we design to better protect objects in a collision?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 11 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 11 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: What materials best reduce the peak forces in a collision?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 12 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 12 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: How (and why) does the structure of a cushioning material affect the peak forces produced in a collision?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 13 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 13 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: How can we use our science ideas and other societal wants and needs to refine our designs?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 14 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 14 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: How can we use what we figured out to evaluate another engineer’s design?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 15 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 15 Teacher Edition.docx

lessons.title: OPTIONAL How can we market our designs to our potential investors?
lessons.teacherEditionPath: Lesson 16 - 8.1 Contact Forces/8.1 Lesson 16 Teacher Edition.docx
